content:
STATEMENT OF WORK CONFIDENTIAL | v2.0
STATEMENT OF WORK
Fractional Chief of Staff Engagement
Effective Date: [DATE] | Version 2.0
ENGAGEMENT OVERVIEW
Client / Company UtopIQ, a Schmidt-Morgan Corporation
Contracting Party Marc Greaves
Role Fractional Chief of Staff
Effective Date 3/30/26
Initial Term Three (3) months — Trial Period ending 6/30/26
Weekly Commitment 10–20 hours per week, flexing up or down as mutually agreed
Compensation Model Non-cash / value-exchange + milestone-based incentives (see Section 6)
1. Background & Purpose
UtopIQ is seeking an experienced fractional Chief of Staff to provide strategic development support
during a critical phase of company growth. This engagement is structured as a three-month Trial
Period designed to accomplish three parallel objectives: (1) accelerate pipeline development and
early adopter acquisition in preparation for a June 2026 implementation launch, (2) support CEO
with submission of grant applications to capitalize on non-dilutive funding opportunities, and (3)
explore the addition of the Contractor's proprietary LLM assessment framework into the company's
service offering — with the potential to formalize a deeper, longer-term partnership.
This SOW reflects a mutual investment model: UtopIQ provides Marc Greaves with a high-visibility
platform, elite incubator access, and a strategic network to advance and validate his LLM
framework; the Contractor brings start-up leadership, ability to execute rapidly to drive immediate
outcomes, and pipeline development expertise at a critical juncture for the company's growth.
2. Scope of Services
The Contractor will provide services across three interconnected areas during the three-month Trial
Period:
2.1 LLM Assessment Deep Dive & Strategic Roadmap (Months 1–2)
During the first two months of the engagement, our Chief Technology Officer and Chief Innovation
Officer will work with contractor to conduct a more in-depth analysis of the contractor’s LLM
assessment framework to establish baseline understanding of the novelty and application potential.
At the conclusion of month two, company will allocate a % of our primary technical workstream to
Fractional Chief of Staff Engagement Page 1
STATEMENT OF WORK CONFIDENTIAL | v2.0
support development of the roadmap to achieve MVP with the potential opportunity of adding the
Contractor's LLM assessment framework to the UtopIQ platform. Specifically, the Company will:
• Conduct a structured review of the Contractor's existing conceptual state LLM assessment
methodology, tooling, and framework architecture
• If there is mutual interest with developing the framework for pilot in Q3, the Company will
support the Contractor with drafting a high-level roadmap for advancing the build, inclusive
of a testing strategy; the Company would provide the infrastructure, data and testing
environment to complete
• Identify the most viable pathways for layering the LLM assessment offering into UtopIQ’s
existing and planned service model
• Design and initiate use of the LLM framework as a pre-sales discovery and qualification tool
— testing its efficacy with Q3 early adopter prospects as part of the pipeline development
process
• Leverage UtopIQ's direct pipeline into the elite incubation program (Capital Factory) to
stress-test the framework in a real-world, high-profile environment; UtopIQ CEO must
remain the face of UtopIQ to Capital Factory, but will include Contractor in activities,
conversations, broker introductions that would be high value to Contractor.
Goal: By the end of Month 2, produce a targeted Q3 roadmap and initial test results that demonstrate
whether and how the LLM assessment framework can be formally incorporated into [Company
Name]'s offering — and establish a foundation for expanded collaboration.
2.2 Pipeline Development & Early Adopter Acquisition
Working in close collaboration with UtopIQ CEO and the Capital Factory network, the Contractor
will:
• Drive targeted outreach to enterprise prospects and channel partnership candidates to build
a qualified pipeline of early adopters
• Identify and qualify a pool of at least three (3) prospects ready and well-suited to serve as
early adopters for the June 2026 implementation launch
• Lead relationship development activities with top-contender organizations, leveraging
Capital Factory resources and network access
• Manage the full LOI-to-contract process, including prospect qualification, follow-up cadence,
document negotiation, and execution through signed agreements with monetary payment
commitments
• Support early adopter onboarding preparation in advance of the June 8, 2026 launch date
2.3 Grant Funding Support
The Contractor will support the team's efforts to secure non-dilutive grant funding, including
activities as assigned and mutually agreed to by UtopIQ CEO and Contractor:
• Reviewing and refining the existing strawman proposal into polished, submission-ready
grant applications
• Researching and identifying relevant grant opportunities aligned with the company's stage
and focus area
• Coordinating cross-functional input and ensuring timely submission of completed
applications
• Tracking submission status and managing follow-up with grant-making organizations
Fractional Chief of Staff Engagement Page 2
STATEMENT OF WORK CONFIDENTIAL | v2.0
2.4 General Chief of Staff Support
As capacity allows and priorities dictate, the Contractor may also assist with:
• Strategic planning and prioritization support for the leadership team
• Preparation of materials for investor, partner, or board-level communications
• Operational coordination to support company scaling efforts
3. Milestones & Deliverables
The following milestones govern the Trial Period. Compensation triggers (detailed in Section 6) are
tied to verified achievement of each milestone. Target dates have been defined; dates must be
achieved unless mutually agreed upon, in advance, and written approval by UtopIQ CEO.
Milestone Target Date Success Metric Compensation
Trigger
LLM assessment high level technical End of Month 2 LOI drafted by Company Access / platform
discovery and overall solution fit & expressing interest and intent value (see Sec. 6)
marketing feasibility (Months 1–2) to support development and
market strategy in Q3
3 qualified early adopter prospects with April 17, 2026 Minimum 3 executed LOIs -
signed LOIs from June-ready clients
Formal early adopter selection May 8, 2026 Final cohort selected and -
complete confirmed
Contracts executed — early adopter May 27, 2026 Signed contracts with 3% of up-front
cohort monetary payment revenue captured
commitments from all early for early adopter
adopters cohort
Early adopter onboarding supported; June 8, 2026 Onboarding underway; at 3% of first $1M
$1M raised (non-dilutive and/or dilutive) least $1M in committed raised if achieved
capital raised by June 8
5 of 10 total LOIs signed June 26, 2026 5+ cumulative executed LOIs $10,000
for CY 2026 pipeline
4. Hours & Availability
This is a fractional engagement. The Contractor will dedicate approximately 10 to 20 hours per
week, with the flexibility to flex up or down based on business priorities and mutual agreement.
During intensive periods — particularly around LOI and contracting deadlines — hours may
temporarily increase beyond 20 with advance coordination and mutual consent.
Material changes to scope that would require a sustained adjustment beyond this range will be
addressed via a written amendment to this SOW.
Fractional Chief of Staff Engagement Page 3
STATEMENT OF WORK CONFIDENTIAL | v2.0
5. Term & Path Forward
5.1 Trial Period
The initial term of this engagement ("Trial Period") commences on [START DATE] and runs for three
(3) months, through [END DATE]. The Trial Period is designed to allow both parties to: (a) evaluate
the working relationship and mutual fit; (b) test and advance the LLM assessment framework in a
live environment; and (c) assess performance against the pipeline milestones outlined in

fit; (b) test and advance the LLM assessment framework in a
live environment; and (c) assess performance against the pipeline milestones outlined in Section 3.
5.2 Path to a Second SOW
At the conclusion of the Trial Period, if milestones have been achieved and both parties are satisfied
with compatibility and overall fit, [Company Name] and the Contractor will engage in good-faith
discussions to execute a Second Statement of Work. The second SOW may include:
• Greater depth of involvement and an expanded strategic role
• Formal incorporation of the LLM assessment framework into [Company Name]'s platform or
service offering
• Continued or enhanced participation in the Capital Factory UtopIQ partnership
• Additional compensation structures, including potential equity consideration, commensurate
with expanded scope and utilization of LLM assessment framework under UtopIQ brand
• Evolution toward a full-time arrangement, if of mutual interest, with competitive
compensation and schedule flexibility
The second SOW will be structured around mutual interest and demonstrated results — not an
automatic continuation. Both parties retain full discretion to proceed or conclude the engagement at
the end of the Trial Period.
6. Compensation
This engagement is structured as a value-exchange model. Rather than traditional cash
compensation during the Trial Period, [Company Name] provides the Contractor with direct access
to a high-value platform, network, and resources that advance the Contractor's own strategic
objectives — specifically, the development, testing, and validation of his proprietary LLM
assessment framework.
6.1 Platform & Network Access (Non-Cash Compensation)
As the primary compensation for services rendered during the Trial Period, [Company Name] will
provide the Contractor with:
VALUE PROVIDED TO CONTRACTOR
🚀 Elite Incubation Direct line into UtopIQ's highly competitive incubator program,
Access providing a structured, high-visibility environment to further build, test,
and refine the LLM assessment framework
Fractional Chief of Staff Engagement Page 4
STATEMENT OF WORK CONFIDENTIAL | v2.0
🧪 Live Testing Use of an active, real-world startup environment with sandbox testing
Platform environment and genuine enterprise prospects to stress-test the LLM
framework as a pre-sales qualification tool
🌐 Network & Full access to UtopIQ’s team member network, partner relationships,
Resources Capital Factory connections, and support resources throughout the
engagement
🎯 Advisor Board Exposure to and engagement with UtopIQ’s established Board of
Visibility Advisors, which includes senior executives across AI, blockchain, and
the public sector
📈 High-Profile Association with a high-visibility, high-profile startup operating at the
Platform intersection of AI and enterprise — providing credibility, case study
material, and strategic positioning for the Contractor's own work
6.2 Milestone-Based Incentive Bonuses
In addition to the platform and network access described above, the Contractor will be eligible for
the following milestone-based incentive bonuses upon verified achievement – see table in section 3
6.3 Independent Contractor — No Base Salary
For the avoidance of doubt, no base salary, hourly rate, or guaranteed cash payment is included in
this SOW during the Trial Period. The Contractor acknowledges that the value provided under

y, hourly rate, or guaranteed cash payment is included in
this SOW during the Trial Period. The Contractor acknowledges that the value provided under
Section 6.1, combined with the milestone incentives under

this SOW during the Trial Period. The Contractor acknowledges that the value provided under
Section 6.1, combined with the milestone incentives under Section 6.2, represents the full
compensation structure for this engagement.
7. Independent Contractor Relationship
The Contractor will be engaged as an independent contractor and not as an employee of [Company
Name]. The Contractor is responsible for all applicable taxes on any cash compensation received.
Nothing in this SOW creates an employment, partnership, or joint venture relationship between the
parties.
8. Confidentiality
Each party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all non-public information received from the
other party in connection with this engagement. This includes, but is not limited to, business
strategies, prospect and client data, LLM framework methodology and architecture, grant materials,
and financial information. This obligation is mutual and survives the termination of this SOW.
9. Intellectual Property
9.1 Work Product
Fractional Chief of Staff Engagement Page 5
STATEMENT OF WORK CONFIDENTIAL | v2.0
All deliverables, materials, and work product created by the Contractor specifically for [Company
Name] in the performance of services under this SOW shall be considered work made for hire and
shall be the sole property of [Company Name].
9.2 LLM Framework — Contractor IP
For the avoidance of doubt, the Contractor's pre-existing LLM assessment framework, methodology,
tools, and related intellectual property ("Contractor IP") remain the sole property of the Contractor.
Any enhancements, adaptations, or derivatives of the Contractor IP developed during this
engagement shall be addressed in the Second SOW or a separate IP agreement, should the parties
elect to proceed with formal integration.
10. Termination
Either party may terminate this SOW with fourteen (14) days written notice. In the event of
termination by [Company Name] without cause, the Contractor shall retain any milestone bonuses
earned through the date of termination. Any milestone bonuses contingent on future events shall be
forfeited upon termination unless otherwise agreed in writing.
11. Governing Law
This SOW shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to conflict of law
principles.
12. Signatures
By signing below, the parties agree to the terms and conditions set forth in this Statement of Work.
[Company Authorized Signatory] Marc [Last Name], Contractor
Name: ________________________________ Name: ________________________________
Title: ________________________________ Date: ________________________________
Date: ________________________________
